differentials.name: Follicular bronchiolitis (← lymphoid hyperplasia): rheumatoid arthritis, Sjögren syndrome
differentials.raw: Follicular bronchiolitis (← lymphoid hyperplasia): rheumatoid arthritis, Sjögren syndrome

differentials.name: Cryptogenic organizing pneumonia = bronchiolitis obliterans with organizing pneumonia (BOOP) = proliferative bronchiolitis
differentials.raw: Cryptogenic organizing pneumonia = bronchiolitis obliterans with organizing pneumonia (BOOP) = proliferative bronchiolitis

differentials.name: Cystic lung disease
differentials.raw: Cystic lung disease (thin septum surrounds area of air attenuation, central vessel not present)

differentials.name: Panlobular emphysema
differentials.raw: Panlobular emphysema (distortion of vascular + septal architecture, bullae)

context: HRCT of Bronchiolitis [CT findings are nonspecific and must be interpreted in the appropriate clinical context] Cause: (a)  infection via endobronchial spread (acute bronchiolitis): ◊  Most common cause of tree-in-bud appearance. ›  bacterial (most common) ›  mycobacterial: classic TB, M. avium-intracellulare ›  viral: acute infectious bronchiolitis in infants and young children due to RSV, adenovirus, mycoplasma ›  parasitic ›  fungal (b)  inhalation of organic / inorganic antigens: hypersensitivity pneumonitis, mineral dust airways disease (c)  immunologic deficiency or impaired host defense
